destinations.evidence: The builder validates the detected async precompile payload with the shared validate_async_payload routine before decoding executor and TTL into a commitment.
The verifier independently enforces TTL bounds, nonzero executor, payload validity, and decoded executor/TTL parity for each async commitment.

equivalence_argument: The builder rejects zero executor payloads via validate_async_payload before creating commitments, while the verifier separately rejects zero executor commitments structurally. Nonzero executor addresses pass this field-level gate and are checked by later registry/state validation.
notes: The current builder returns an error rather than silently creating a commitment when executor is zero; this is equivalent to skipping inclusion of an invalid async commitment.
rationale: The detector gains the zero-address executor check and the new unit/property tests define equivalence with verifier rejection for zero executor only. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The async detector must return None and skip creating an async commitment when executor_address is Address::ZERO, while nonzero executor addresses are not rejected solely by this validation, matching verifier executor validation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run detector and verifier validation with Address::ZERO, Address::repeat_byte(0x42), and fuzzed 20-byte addresses; assert both reject only the zero address under this rule.
summary: Builder and verifier reject zero executor commitments.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run detector and verifier validation with Address::ZERO, Address::repeat_byte(0x42), and fuzzed 20-byte addresses; assert both reject only the zero address under this rule.
title: Builder skips zero executor commitments
